client.c(3) Library Functions Manual client.c(3)

NAME

client.c - Client-side handshake protocol implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Process server's public key and send our public key. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Process auth challenge and send response. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Wait for handshake complete confirmation. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Key exchange using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Auth response using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients only).

Detailed Description

Client-side handshake protocol implementation.

Definition in file lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t payload_len)

Client: Process auth challenge and send response.

Parameters

ctx Handshake context (must be in CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE state)
transport ACIP transport to send on
packet_type Packet type received (should be PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE)
payload Received packet payload
payload_len Length of received payload

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Client processes server's AUTH_CHALLENGE packet and sends AUTH_RESPONSE. Generates HMAC bound to shared secret using password or client key.

Note

Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling this function

State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE -> CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING

Definition at line 613 of file lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.

615                                                                             {
616   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE) {
617     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
618   }
619   if (!transport) {
620     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
621   }
622 
623   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
624   int result;
625 
626   // If server sent HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, authentication was skipped (client has
627   // no key)
628   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
629     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
630     ctx->crypto_ctx.handshake_complete = true; // Mark crypto context as ready for rekeying
631     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication required)");
632     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
633   }
634 
635   // If server sent AUTH_FAILED, client is not authorized
636   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED) {
637     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server rejected authentication - client key not authorized");
638   }
639 
640   // Otherwise, verify packet type is AUTH_CHALLENGE
641   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE) {
642     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
643                      "Expected AUTH_CHALLENGE, HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, or AUTH_FAILED, "
644                      "got packet type %d",
645                      packet_type);
646   }
647 
648   // Validate packet size using session parameters
649   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
650       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE, payload_len);
651   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
652     return validation_result;
653   }
654 
655   // Parse auth requirement flags
656   uint8_t auth_flags = payload[0];
657 
658   // Copy nonce to local buffer before freeing payload
659   // Use auth_challenge_size since that's what the server sent
660   // Note: auth_challenge_size is uint8_t (max 255), buffer is 256 bytes, so always sufficient
661   uint8_t nonce_buffer[256];
662   memcpy(nonce_buffer, payload + 1, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
663   const uint8_t *nonce = nonce_buffer;
664 
665   log_debug("Server auth requirements: password=%s, client_key=%s",
666             (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD) ? "required" : "no",
667             (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY) ? "required" : "no");
668 
669   // Check if we can satisfy the server's authentication requirements
670   bool has_password = ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password;
671   bool has_client_key = (ctx->client_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
672   bool password_required = (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD);
673   bool client_key_required = (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY);
674 
675   // Provide specific error messages based on what's missing
676   if (password_required && !has_password) {
677     if (client_key_required && !has_client_key) {
678       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires both password and client key authentication. Please "
679                                      "provide --password and --key to authenticate");
680     }
681     // Prompt for password interactively
682     char prompted_password[PASSWORD_BUFFER_SIZE];
683     if (prompt_password("Server password required - please enter password:", prompted_password,
684                         sizeof(prompted_password)) != 0) {
685       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to read password");
686     }
687 
688     // Derive password key from prompted password
689     log_debug("Deriving key from prompted password");
690     crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, prompted_password);
691     sodium_memzero(prompted_password, sizeof(prompted_password));
692 
693     if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
694       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
695     }
696 
697     // Mark that password auth is now available
698     ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
699     has_password = true; // Update flag for logic below
700   }
701 
702   // Authentication response priority:
703   // NOTE: Identity verification happens during KEY_EXCHANGE phase, not
704   // AUTH_RESPONSE!
705   // 1. If server requires password → MUST send HMAC (hmac_size bytes), error if no password
706   // 2. Else if server requires identity (whitelist) → MUST send Ed25519 signature (signature_size bytes), error if no
707   // key
708   // 3. Else if client has password → send HMAC (optional password auth)
709   // 4. Else if client has SSH key → send Ed25519 signature (optional identity
710   // proof)
711   // 5. Else → no authentication available
712 
713   // Clean up payload before any early returns
714   if (password_required) {
715     // Server requires password - HIGHEST PRIORITY
716     // (Identity was already verified in KEY_EXCHANGE phase if whitelist is
717     // enabled)
718     if (!has_password) {
719       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires password authentication\n"
720                                      "Please provide --password for this server");
721     }
722 
723     result = send_password_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "required password");
724     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
725       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send password auth response");
726       return result;
727     }
728   } else if (client_key_required) {
729     // Server requires client key (whitelist) - SECOND PRIORITY
730     if (!has_client_key) {
731       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires client key authentication (whitelist)\n"
732                                      "Please provide --key with your authorized Ed25519 key");
733     }
734 
735     result = send_key_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "required client key");
736     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
737       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send key auth response");
738       return result;
739     }
740   } else if (has_password) {
741     // No server requirements, but client has password → send HMAC + client
742     // nonce (optional)
743     result = send_password_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "optional password");
744     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
745       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send password auth response");
746       return result;
747     }
748   } else if (has_client_key) {
749     // No server requirements, but client has SSH key → send Ed25519 signature +
750     // client nonce (optional)
751     result = send_key_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "optional identity");
752     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
753       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send key auth response");
754       return result;
755     }
756   } else {
757     // No authentication method available
758     // Continue without authentication (server will decide if this is
759     // acceptable)
760     log_debug("No authentication credentials provided - continuing without "
761               "authentication");
762   }
763 
764   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING;
765 
766   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
767 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY, AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_derive_password_key(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    crypto_context_t::handshake_complete,
    crypto_context_t::has_password, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    log_debug, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, PASSWORD_BUFFER_SIZE,
    prompt_password(), SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and
  private_key_t::type.


Referenced by client_handle_auth_challenge(), and
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket().






asciichat_error_t
  crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Auth response using socket (TCP clients only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_auth_response() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 910 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


911                                                                                        {
912   // Receive packet using old method
913   packet_type_t packet_type;
914   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
915   size_t payload_len = 0;
916   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
917   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
918     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive packet from server");
919   }
920 
921   // Create temporary TCP transport
922   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
923   if (!temp_transport) {
924     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
925   }
926 
927   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
928   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
929       crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
930 
931   // Destroy temporary transport
932   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
933 
934   return handshake_result;
935 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(), ERROR_NETWORK,
    receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by client_crypto_handshake(), and
    discovery_session_start().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Client: Wait for handshake complete confirmation.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING state)


transport ACIP transport (unused, for consistency)


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Client processes server's SERVER_AUTH_RESP or AUTH_FAILED packet.
    After receiving, handshake is complete and encryption is ready.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY




Definition at line 769 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


771                                                                        {
772   // Accept both KEY_EXCHANGE and AUTHENTICATING states for simple mode compatibility
773   // In simple mode, server skips AUTH_CHALLENGE and sends HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE directly
774   if (!ctx || (ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE && ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING)) {
775     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
776     return ERROR_INVALID_STATE;
777   }
778   if (!transport) {
779     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
780   }
781 
782   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
783   (void)transport; // Unused parameter (this function only receives, doesn't send)
784 
785   // Check packet type
786   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED) {
787     // Parse the auth failure packet to get specific reasons
788     if (payload_len >= sizeof(auth_failure_packet_t)) {
789       auth_failure_packet_t *failure = (auth_failure_packet_t *)payload;
790       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Server rejected authentication:");
791 
792       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) {
793         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Incorrect password");
794       }
795       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED) {
796         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Server requires a password (use --password)");
797       }
798       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED) {
799         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Server requires a whitelisted client key (use --key "
800                                      "with your SSH key)");
801       }
802       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED) {
803         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Your client key is not in the server's whitelist");
804       }
805       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID) {
806         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Client signature verification failed");
807       }
808 
809       // Provide helpful guidance
810       if (failure->reason_flags & (AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT | AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED)) {
811         if ((failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) &&
812             (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED)) {
813           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Server requires BOTH correct password AND "
814                                        "whitelisted key");
815         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) {
816           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Check your password and try again");
817         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED) {
818           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Provide your SSH key with --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519");
819         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED) {
820           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Your key needs to be added to the server's whitelist");
821         }
822       }
823     } else {
824       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Server rejected authentication (no details provided)");
825     }
826     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
827                      "Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC"); // Special code for
828                                                                        // auth failure - do
829                                                                        // not retry
830   }
831 
832   // Handle no-auth flow: server sends HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE directly
833   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
834     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
835     log_info("Handshake complete (no authentication required)");
836     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
837   }
838 
839   // Handle with-auth flow: server sends SERVER_AUTH_RESP after authentication
840   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP) {
841     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
842                      "Expected HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE, or AUTH_FAILED, got packet type %d",
843                      packet_type);
844   }
845 
846   // Verify server's HMAC for mutual authentication
847   // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size (negotiated during handshake) rather than SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE_SIZE constant
848   if (payload_len != ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size) {
849     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE size: %zu bytes (expected %u)", payload_len,
850                      ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
851   }
852 
853   // Verify server's HMAC (binds to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM)
854   if (!crypto_verify_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload)) {
855     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "SECURITY: Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC");
856     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "This may indicate a man-in-the-middle attack!");
857     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
858                      "Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC"); // Authentication
859                                                                        // failure - do not
860                                                                        // retry
861   }
862   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
863   log_info("Server authentication successful - mutual authentication complete");
864 
865   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
866 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED, AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT, AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_challenge_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_verify_auth_response(), ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::hmac_size,
    log_info, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags, SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state.

    crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients
    only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_complete() with
  acip_transport_t instead

    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


940                                                                                                                    {
941   // Receive packet using old method
942   packet_type_t packet_type;
943   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
944   size_t payload_len = 0;
945   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
946   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
947     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive handshake completion packet");
948   }
949 
950   // Create temporary TCP transport
951   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
952   if (!temp_transport) {
953     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
954   }
955 
956   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
957   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
958       crypto_handshake_client_complete(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
959 
960   // Destroy temporary transport
961   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
962 
963   return handshake_result;
964 }

Referenced by client_crypto_handshake(), and
    discovery_session_start().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Client: Process server's public key and send our public key.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Client processes server's KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet and responds
    with KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP. Supports both simple and authenticated formats.
    Verifies server signature if present.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE




Definition at line 31 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


33                                                                            {
34   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT) {
35     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
36   }
37   if (!transport) {
38     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
39   }
40 
41   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
42   int result;
43 
44   // Verify packet type
45   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT) {
46     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, got packet type %d", packet_type);
47   }
48 
49   log_debug("CLIENT_KEY_EXCHANGE: Received packet with payload_len=%zu, kex_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u",
50             payload_len, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size,
51             ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
52 
53   // Check payload size - only authenticated format supported
54   // Authenticated: public_key_size + auth_public_key_size + signature_size bytes
55   size_t expected_auth_size =
56       ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
57 
58   uint8_t *server_ephemeral_key;
59   // Use the crypto context's public key size to ensure compatibility
60   size_t key_size = sizeof(ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key);
61   server_ephemeral_key = SAFE_MALLOC(key_size, uint8_t *);
62   if (!server_ephemeral_key) {
63     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for server ephemeral key");
64   }
65   uint8_t *server_identity_key;
66   server_identity_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, uint8_t *);
67   uint8_t *server_signature;
68   server_signature = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size, uint8_t *);
69 
70   if (!server_identity_key || !server_signature) {
71     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
72     if (server_identity_key)
73       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
74     if (server_signature)
75       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
76     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for server identity key or signature");
77   }
78 
79   // Validate packet size using session parameters
80   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
81       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, payload_len);
82   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
83     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
84     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
85     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
86     return validation_result;
87   }
88 
89   // Check if server is using authenticated format (includes signature)
90   // This covers both cases:
91   // 1. With identity key: ephemeral + identity + signature
92   // 2. Without identity key but with signature: ephemeral + 0 + signature
93   if (payload_len == expected_auth_size) {
94     // Authenticated format:
95     // [ephemeral:public_key_size][identity:auth_public_key_size][signature:signature_size]
96     log_debug("Received authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes)", expected_auth_size);
97     memcpy(server_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
98     memcpy(server_identity_key, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
99     memcpy(server_signature, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size,
100            ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
101 
102     // Server is using client authentication
103     ctx->server_uses_client_auth = true;
104 
105     // DEBUG: Print identity key received
106     char hex_id[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
107     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
108       safe_snprintf(hex_id + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_identity_key[i]);
109     }
110     hex_id[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
111     log_debug("Received identity key: %s", hex_id);
112 
113     // DEBUG: Print ephemeral key and signature
114     char hex_eph[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
115     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
116       safe_snprintf(hex_eph + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_ephemeral_key[i]);
117     }
118     hex_eph[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
119     log_debug("Received ephemeral key: %s", hex_eph);
120 
121     char hex_sig[HEX_STRING_SIZE_64];
122     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE; i++) {
123       safe_snprintf(hex_sig + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_signature[i]);
124     }
125     hex_sig[HEX_STRING_SIZE_64 - 1] = '\0';
126     log_debug("Received signature: %s", hex_sig);
127 
128     // Verify signature: server identity signed the ephemeral key
129     log_debug("Verifying server's signature over ephemeral key (already logged above)");
130 
131     // If client didn't specify --server-key, skip signature verification
132     // (client doesn't care about server identity verification)
133     if (!ctx->verify_server_key) {
134       log_info("Skipping server signature verification (no --server-key specified)");
135       log_warn("Connection is encrypted but server identity is NOT verified (vulnerable to MITM)");
136     } else {
137       // Extract GPG key ID from expected_server_key if it's a GPG key (gpg:KEYID format)
138       const char *gpg_key_id = NULL;
139       if (ctx->expected_server_key[0] != '\0' && strncmp(ctx->expected_server_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
140         const char *key_id_start = ctx->expected_server_key + 4;
141         size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id_start);
142         // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short, long, or full fingerprint)
143         if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
144           gpg_key_id = key_id_start;
145           log_debug("Using GPG key ID from --server-key for verification: %s", gpg_key_id);
146         }
147       }
148 
149       if (ed25519_verify_signature(server_identity_key, server_ephemeral_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
150                                    server_signature, gpg_key_id) != 0) {
151         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
152         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
153         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
154         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server signature verification FAILED - rejecting connection. "
155                                        "This indicates: Server's identity key does not "
156                                        "match its ephemeral key, Potential man-in-the-middle attack, "
157                                        "Corrupted or malicious server");
158       }
159       log_debug("Server signature verified successfully");
160     }
161 
162     // Verify server identity against expected key if --server-key is specified
163     if (ctx->verify_server_key && strlen(ctx->expected_server_key) > 0) {
164       // Parse ALL expected server keys (github:/gitlab: may have multiple keys)
165       public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
166       size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
167       if (parse_public_keys(ctx->expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) != 0 ||
168           num_expected_keys == 0) {
169         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
170         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
171         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
172         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
173                          "Failed to parse expected server key: %s. Check that "
174                          "--server-key value is valid (ssh-ed25519 "
175                          "format, github:username, or hex)",
176                          ctx->expected_server_key);
177       }
178 
179       // Compare server's IDENTITY key against ALL expected keys (match any one)
180       // This supports users with multiple SSH keys (e.g., different machines)
181       bool key_matched = false;
182       for (size_t i = 0; i < num_expected_keys; i++) {
183         if (sodium_memcmp(server_identity_key, expected_keys[i].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
184           key_matched = true;
185           log_debug("Server identity key matched expected key %zu/%zu", i + 1, num_expected_keys);
186           break;
187         }
188       }
189 
190       if (!key_matched) {
191         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
192         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
193         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
194         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO,
195                          "Server identity key mismatch - potential MITM attack! "
196                          "Expected key(s) from: %s (checked %zu keys), Server presented a different key "
197                          "than specified with --server-key, DO NOT CONNECT to this "
198                          "server - likely man-in-the-middle attack!",
199                          ctx->expected_server_key, num_expected_keys);
200       }
201       log_info("Server identity key verified against --server-key (%zu key(s) checked)", num_expected_keys);
202     }
203 
204     // Note: Server IP resolution now handled by caller (TCP transport layer)
205     // For WebSocket/WebRTC transports, server_ip should be set by the transport
206     // before handshake begins. TCP clients will use the legacy wrapper which
207     // handles this.
208     if (ctx->server_ip[0] == '\0') {
209       log_debug("Server IP not set - skipping known_hosts verification (non-TCP transport)");
210     } else {
211       log_debug("Server IP already set: %s", ctx->server_ip);
212     }
213 
214     // Check known_hosts for this server (if we have server IP and port)
215     // Check if known_hosts verification should be skipped
216     bool skip_known_hosts = false;
217     const char *env_skip = platform_getenv("ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK");
218     if (env_skip && strcmp(env_skip, STR_ONE) == 0) {
219       log_warn(
220           "Skipping known_hosts checking for authenticated connection (ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK=1)");
221       skip_known_hosts = true;
222     }
223 #ifndef NDEBUG
224     // In debug builds, also skip for Claude Code (LLM automation can't do interactive prompts)
225     else if (platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE")) {
226       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking (CLAUDECODE set in debug build)");
227       skip_known_hosts = true;
228     }
229 #endif
230 
231     if (!skip_known_hosts && ctx->server_ip[0] != '\0' && ctx->server_port > 0) {
232       asciichat_error_t known_host_result = check_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key);
233       if (known_host_result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION) {
234         // Key mismatch - MITM attack detected! Prompt user for confirmation
235         log_error("SECURITY: Server key does NOT match known_hosts entry!\n"
236                   "This indicates a possible man-in-the-middle attack!");
237         uint8_t stored_key[ZERO_KEY_SIZE] = {0}; // We don't have the stored key easily
238                                                  // accessible, use zeros for now
239         if (!display_mitm_warning(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, stored_key, server_identity_key)) {
240           // User declined to continue - ABORT connection for security          SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
241           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
242           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
243           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION,
244                            "SECURITY: Connection aborted - server key mismatch (possible MITM attack)");
245         }
246         // User accepted the risk - continue with connection
247         log_warn("SECURITY WARNING: User accepted MITM risk - continuing with connection");
248       } else if (known_host_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
249         // Unknown host (first connection) - prompt user to verify fingerprint
250         if (!prompt_unknown_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key)) {
251           // User declined to add host - ABORT connection          SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
252           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
253           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
254           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "User declined to verify unknown host");
255         }
256 
257         // User accepted - add to known_hosts
258         if (add_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
259           SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
260           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
261           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
262           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
263                            "CRITICAL SECURITY ERROR: Failed to create known_hosts "
264                            "file! This is a security vulnerability - the "
265                            "program cannot track known hosts. Please check file "
266                            "permissions and ensure the program can write to: %s",
267                            get_known_hosts_path());
268         }
269         log_debug("Server host added to known_hosts successfully");
270       } else if (known_host_result == 1) {
271         // Key matches - connection is secure!
272         log_info("Server host key verified from known_hosts - connection secure");
273       } else {
274         // Unexpected error code from check_known_host        SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
275         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
276         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
277         return SET_ERRNO(known_host_result, "SECURITY: known_hosts verification failed with error code %d",
278                          known_host_result);
279       }
280     }
281   } else if (payload_len == ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size) {
282     // Simple format: just ephemeral key (no identity key)
283     log_debug("Received simple KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes) - server has no "
284               "identity key",
285               payload_len);
286     memcpy(server_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
287 
288     // Clear identity key and signature for simple format
289     memset(server_identity_key, 0, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
290     memset(server_signature, 0, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
291 
292     // Server is not using client authentication in simple mode
293     ctx->server_uses_client_auth = false;
294 
295     log_debug("Received ephemeral key (simple format)");
296 
297     // SECURITY: For servers without identity keys, we implement a different security model:
298     // 1. Verify IP address matches known_hosts entry
299     // 2. Always require user confirmation (no silent connections)
300     // 3. Store server fingerprint for future verification
301 
302     if (ctx->server_ip[0] == '\0' || ctx->server_port <= 0) {
303       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
304       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
305       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
306       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server IP or port not set, cannot check known_hosts");
307     }
308 
309     // Check if this server was previously connected to (IP verification)
310     bool skip_known_hosts = false;
311     asciichat_error_t known_host_result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
312     const char *env_skip_known_hosts_checking = platform_getenv("ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK");
313     if (env_skip_known_hosts_checking && strcmp(env_skip_known_hosts_checking, STR_ONE) == 0) {
314       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking. This is a security vulnerability.");
315       skip_known_hosts = true;
316     }
317 #ifndef NDEBUG
318     // In debug builds, also skip for Claude Code (LLM automation can't do interactive prompts)
319     else if (platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE")) {
320       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking (CLAUDECODE set in debug build).");
321       skip_known_hosts = true;
322     }
323 #endif
324     else {
325       known_host_result = check_known_host_no_identity(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port);
326     }
327 
328     if (skip_known_hosts || known_host_result == 1) {
329       // Server IP is known and verified - allow connection without warnings
330       log_info("SECURITY: Server IP %s:%u is known (no-identity entry found) - connection verified", ctx->server_ip,
331                ctx->server_port);
332     } else if (known_host_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
333       // Server IP is unknown - require user confirmation
334       log_warn("SECURITY: Unknown server IP %s:%u with no identity key\n"
335                "This connection is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks\n"
336                "Anyone can intercept your connection and read your data",
337                ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port);
338 
339       if (!prompt_unknown_host_no_identity(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port)) {
340         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
341         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
342         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
343         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "User declined to connect to unknown server without identity key");
344       }
345 
346       // User accepted - add to known_hosts as no-identity entry
347       // For servers without identity keys, pass zero key to indicate no-identity
348       uint8_t zero_key[ZERO_KEY_SIZE] = {0};
349       if (add_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, zero_key) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
350         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
351         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
352         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
353         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
354                          "CRITICAL SECURITY ERROR: Failed to create known_hosts "
355                          "file! This is a security vulnerability - the "
356                          "program cannot track known hosts. Please check file "
357                          "permissions and ensure the program can write to: %s",
358                          get_known_hosts_path());
359       }
360       log_debug("Server host added to known_hosts successfully");
361     } else if (known_host_result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION) {
362       // Server previously had identity key but now has none - potential security issue
363       log_warn("SECURITY: Server previously had identity key but now has none - potential security issue");
364       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
365       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
366       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
367       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION, "Server key configuration changed - potential security issue");
368     } else {
369       // Other error checking known_hosts (e.g., ERROR_INVALID_PARAM)      SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
370       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
371       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
372       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to verify server IP address");
373     }
374   } else {
375     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
376     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
377     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
378     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
379                      "Invalid KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT size: %zu bytes (expected %zu or "
380                      "%zu). This indicates: Protocol violation "
381                      "or incompatible server version, Potential man-in-the-middle "
382                      "attack, Network corruption",
383                      payload_len, expected_auth_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
384     // retry
385   }
386 
387   // Set peer's public key (EPHEMERAL X25519) - this also derives the shared secret
388   crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_set_peer_public_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, server_ephemeral_key);
389   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
390     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
391     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
392     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
393     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to set peer public key and derive shared secret: %s",
394                      crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
395   }
396 
397   // Determine if client has an identity key
398   bool client_has_identity_key = (ctx->client_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
399 
400   // Send authenticated response if server has identity key (auth_public_key_size > 0)
401   // OR if server requires client authentication (require_client_auth)
402   // Note: server_uses_client_auth is set when server has identity key, but we should
403   // send authenticated response when server has identity key regardless of client auth requirement
404   bool server_has_identity = (ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size > 0 && ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size > 0);
405   bool server_requires_auth = server_has_identity || ctx->require_client_auth;
406 
407   if (server_requires_auth) {
408     // Send authenticated packet:
409     // [ephemeral:kex_size][identity:auth_size][signature:sig_size][gpg_key_id_len:1][gpg_key_id:0-16]
410     // Use Ed25519 sizes since client has Ed25519 key
411     size_t ed25519_pubkey_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // Ed25519 public key is always 32 bytes
412     size_t ed25519_sig_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;     // Ed25519 signature is always 64 bytes
413     size_t response_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ed25519_pubkey_size + ed25519_sig_size;
414 
415     // Check if client has a GPG key ID to send
416     uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = 0;
417     if (ctx->client_gpg_key_id[0] != '\0') {
418       gpg_key_id_len = (uint8_t)strlen(ctx->client_gpg_key_id);
419       if (gpg_key_id_len > 40) {
420         gpg_key_id_len = 40; // Truncate to max length (full fingerprint)
421       }
422       response_size += 1 + gpg_key_id_len; // 1 byte for length + key ID
423     } else {
424       response_size += 1; // Just the length byte (0)
425     }
426 
427     uint8_t *key_response = SAFE_MALLOC(response_size, uint8_t *);
428     size_t offset = 0;
429 
430     // Copy ephemeral key
431     memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
432            ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size); // X25519 ephemeral for encryption
433     offset += ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
434 
435     if (client_has_identity_key) {
436       // Client has identity key - send it with signature
437       memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->client_private_key.public_key, ed25519_pubkey_size); // Ed25519 identity
438       offset += ed25519_pubkey_size;
439 
440       // Sign ephemeral key with client identity key
441       if (ed25519_sign_message(&ctx->client_private_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
442                                key_response + offset) != 0) {
443         SAFE_FREE(key_response);
444         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
445         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
446         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
447         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to sign client ephemeral key");
448       }
449       offset += ed25519_sig_size;
450     } else {
451       // Client has no identity key - send null identity and null signature
452       memset(key_response + offset, 0, ed25519_pubkey_size); // Null identity
453       offset += ed25519_pubkey_size;
454       memset(key_response + offset, 0, ed25519_sig_size); // Null signature
455       offset += ed25519_sig_size;
456     }
457 
458     // Append GPG key ID length
459     key_response[offset] = gpg_key_id_len;
460     offset += 1;
461 
462     // Append GPG key ID if present
463     if (gpg_key_id_len > 0) {
464       memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->client_gpg_key_id, gpg_key_id_len);
465       offset += gpg_key_id_len;
466       log_debug("Including client GPG key ID in KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE: %.*s", gpg_key_id_len, ctx->client_gpg_key_id);
467     }
468 
469     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, key_response, response_size, 0);
470     if (result != 0) {
471       SAFE_FREE(key_response);
472       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
473       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
474       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
475       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE packet");
476     }
477 
478     // Zero out the buffer before freeing
479     sodium_memzero(key_response, response_size);
480     SAFE_FREE(key_response);
481   } else {
482     // Send X25519 encryption key only to server (no identity key)
483     // Format: [X25519 pubkey (kex_size)] = kex_size bytes total
484     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
485                                        ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, 0);
486     if (result != 0) {
487       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
488       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
489       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
490       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE packet");
491     }
492   }
493 
494   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE;
495 
496   // Free temporary buffers before successful return
497   SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
498   SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
499   SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
500 
501   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
502 }






References add_known_host(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size, check_known_host(),
    check_known_host_no_identity(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_gpg_key_id,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_set_peer_public_key(),
    display_mitm_warning(), ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
    ed25519_sign_message(), ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::expected_server_key,
    get_known_hosts_path(), HEX_STRING_SIZE_32,
    HEX_STRING_SIZE_64, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn, MAX_CLIENTS,
    packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, parse_public_keys(),
    platform_getenv(), prompt_unknown_host(),
    prompt_unknown_host_no_identity(),
    crypto_context_t::public_key, private_key_t::public_key,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_ip,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_port,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_uses_client_auth,
    SET_ERRNO, crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, STR_ONE,
    private_key_t::type,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::verify_server_key, and
    ZERO_KEY_SIZE.


Referenced by client_handle_key_exchange_init(), and
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket().






asciichat_error_t
  crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t
  * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Key exchange using socket (TCP clients only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 877 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


877                                                                                                                        {
878   if (!ctx) {
879     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Context cannot be NULL");
880   }
881 
882   // Receive packet using old method
883   packet_type_t packet_type;
884   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
885   size_t payload_len = 0;
886   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
887   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
888     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet");
889   }
890 
891   // Create temporary TCP transport
892   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
893   if (!temp_transport) {
894     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
895   }
896 
897   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
898   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
899       crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
900 
901   // Destroy temporary transport
902   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
903 
904   return handshake_result;
905 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK, receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by client_crypto_handshake(), and
    discovery_session_start().
